Joshua Salako

M.Sc. Graduate, Google DeepMind Scholar

Stellenbosch University & AIMS South Africa

salakojoshua1234[at]gmail[dot]com

Hi 👋🏿 I recently completed my master's in AI for Science at AIMS South Africa and Stellenbosch University, graduating with distinction. I am grateful to have been supervised by Dr Lorène Jeantet. I work on machine learning for ecology—mostly computer vision for making sense of camera-trap data.

For my master's, I taught models to spot endangered Western Leopard Toads across 1.2 million camera-trap images, then used those detections to test whether under-road tunnels actually help the toads avoid roadkill. A very interesting project, and perhaps the most consequential game of “find the toad” I will ever play. I am now working with the Machine Learning for Ecology group at AIMS and looking for PhD opportunities—very open to conversations!

I am also curious about agentic systems. Alongside research, I am an AI/ML Engineer at Rio Eigen (industry experience pays too—quite literally). Away from models, you will usually find me behind a camera or somewhere inside a good playlist.

Selected Publications

Fish-NET: Advancing Aquaculture Management through AI-Enhanced Fish Monitoring and Tracking

Joshua Salako, Foluso Ojo, Olushina Olawale Awe

AGRIS on-line Papers in Economics and Informatics, 16(2), 121–131, 2024.

What it does

Tests modern object detectors on catfish-pond imagery, then pairs the strongest model with tracking to automate fish monitoring for aquaculture managers.

  • Publication
  • DOI

A Comparative Exploration of SHAP and LIME for Enhancing the Interpretability of Machine Learning Models in Obesity Prediction

Olushina Olawale Awe, Joshua Salako, Paulo Canas Rodrigues, Natisha Dukhi, Ronaldo Dias

Practical Statistical Learning and Data Science Methods, Springer, 253–281, 2024.

What it does

Compares two explanation methods, SHAP and LIME, to show how obesity-prediction models reach their decisions and where their explanations differ.

  • Publication
  • DOI

All publications →

News

  1. Jul 2026

    Defended my master's thesis on Western Leopard Toads in 1.2 million camera-trap images—and graduated with distinction.

  2. Jan 2026

    Released Latent Geometry of Taste, my recommender-systems preprint built on 32 million movie ratings.

  3. Dec 2025

    Joined the Machine Learning for Ecology group at AIMS to build computer-vision tools for wildlife monitoring.

  4. Dec 2025

    Co-authored a data-driven framework for spotting at-risk students in Nigerian secondary schools.
